title: Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900)
description: Build foundational knowledge of Azure cloud services and core concepts
content: Comprehensive fundamentals training covering Azure core services, security, privacy, compliance, and pricing.

learningObjectives.title: Cloud Computing Concepts
learningObjectives.description: Understand cloud computing principles, deployment models, and service types

learningObjectives.title: Azure Core Services
learningObjectives.description: Describe Azure core services including compute, networking, storage, and databases

learningObjectives.title: Security & Compliance
learningObjectives.description: Describe Azure security, privacy, compliance, and trust features

instructor.name: Yaïr Knijn
instructor.title: Principal Cloud Architect & Engineer
instructor.bio: Experienced Microsoft Certified Trainer specializing in Azure cloud technologies and solutions architecture.

overview: This comprehensive Azure Fundamentals training provides a solid foundation for understanding Microsoft Azure cloud services. Designed for beginners, this course covers all the essential concepts needed to start working with Azure confidently.

whatYouWillLearn.title: Azure Solutions
whatYouWillLearn.description: IoT, AI, machine learning, and serverless computing

whatYouWillLearn.title: Azure Management Tools
whatYouWillLearn.description: Portal, CLI, PowerShell, and ARM templates

whatYouWillLearn.title: Security & Compliance
whatYouWillLearn.description: Identity management, security center, and governance

whatYouWillLearn.title: Pricing & Support
whatYouWillLearn.description: Cost management, service level agreements, and support options

modules.title: Module 1: Cloud Concepts
modules.description: Understanding cloud computing principles, deployment models, and service types
topics: Cloud computing, the shared responsibility model, and consumption-based pricing
Public, private, and hybrid cloud models, with use cases for each
Benefits of high availability, scalability, and reliability in the cloud
IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS service types and when to choose each
Serverless computing

modules.title: Module 2: Core Azure Services
modules.description: Compute, networking, storage, and database services overview
topics: Azure regions, region pairs, availability zones, and datacenters
Resource groups, subscriptions, and management groups
Compute options: virtual machines, containers, and Azure Functions
Virtual networking, VPN Gateway, and ExpressRoute
Azure storage services, access tiers, and redundancy options

modules.title: Module 3: Security & Compliance
modules.description: Azure security features, identity management, and compliance frameworks
topics: Microsoft Entra ID, authentication, and multifactor authentication (MFA)
Conditional Access and role-based access control (RBAC)
Zero Trust and the defense-in-depth model
Microsoft Defender for Cloud
Azure Policy, resource locks, and Microsoft Purview

modules.title: Module 4: Pricing & Support
modules.description: Cost management, pricing models, and Azure support options
topics: Factors that affect cost and the Azure pricing calculator
Cost management capabilities and resource tags
Managing resources with the Azure portal, Cloud Shell, CLI, and PowerShell
Azure Resource Manager templates and infrastructure as code
Monitoring with Azure Advisor, Azure Service Health, and Azure Monitor

handsOnLabs.title: Azure Portal Navigation
handsOnLabs.description: Navigate and explore the Azure portal interface

handsOnLabs.title: Resource Group Management
handsOnLabs.description: Create and manage resource groups in Azure

handsOnLabs.title: Virtual Machine Deployment
handsOnLabs.description: Deploy a basic virtual machine and explore compute options

handsOnLabs.title: Storage Account Setup
handsOnLabs.description: Create storage accounts and understand storage services

targetAudience: IT professionals new to cloud computing

personalStory.story: As someone who has guided hundreds of professionals into Azure, I've seen firsthand how the right foundation makes all the difference. I remember working with a team of traditional infrastructure engineers who were skeptical about cloud. After this training, they successfully migrated their first workloads to Azure and became cloud advocates. This course distills everything I wish I knew when I started with Azure.
